learning_outcomes.description: Identify, assess, and address common data issues including data types, missing values, noise, and outliers.
Perform explanatory data analysis to summarise data and uncover meaningful patterns and relationships.
Develop and interpret regression models for inference and prediction.
Analyse spatial data and account for spatial structure in data-driven analysis.
Apply clustering and data reduction techniques to identify structure in high-dimensional datasets.
Select, implement, and compare machine learning models for prediction and pattern recognition.
Effectively interpret the results and communicate outcomes to the general public as well as experts in the field using a variety of professional communication styles.

assessments.assessment_title: Computer Exercise on data processing and modelling
assessments.task_description:
This assessment will require you to process a data set using the techniques introduced in the lectures and then develop a regression model using the processed data.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment will require you to explore patterns in a data set using the data-driven methods introduced in the lectures.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessments.task_description:
You are expected to actively participate in lectures throughout the semester. In-class quizzes will contribute to your final marks, with points accruing from Week 1 through to Week 13. The full marks for each quiz are 1.25, and your final score will be calculated based on your best 8 out of 12 quizzes. To ensure your quiz is counted, it must be submitted via Blackboard before the end of each lecture.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit it to the Blackboard by the end of each lecture.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
All the topics and learning objectives of this course will be covered in the final exam. This exam will consist of MCQ, short answer, and problem solving. Some of the MCQ problems may require some calculations. Some short answer questions will require calculations. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
